name: Armenteira to Meaño — circular by the Molinos río de Chanca
name_original: Armenteira a Meaño — circular por los Molinos río de Chanca
region_intro: Hiking around Meis, located in Galicia, Spain, offers a diverse landscape characterized by river valleys, forested areas, and moderate elevations. The region features a network of trails that often follow waterways, leading through woodlands and past historical sites such as monasteries and traditional mills. These routes provide opportunities to explore the natural and cultural heritage of the area, with varied terrain suitable for different hiking preferences. Best hiking trails around Meis The most popular hiking route is El Bosque de Colón to Alto de Castrove — circular, a 6.4 miles (10.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 58 minutes to complete. This route offers a circular path through forested areas and ascends to a local high point. Another top favourite among local hikers is Armenteira to Meaño — circular by the Molinos río de Chanca, a moderate 9.6 miles (15.5 km) path. This trail follows the Chanca river, passing by historic mills and offering scenic views of the river valley. Local hikers also love the Armenteira River – Armenteira Monastery loop from A Armenteira, a 7.6 miles (12.3 km) trail leading through riverine landscapes and to the Armenteira Monastery, often completed in about 3 hours 27 minutes. Hiking around Meis is defined by river valleys, forested hills, and cultural landmarks. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult trails, catering to various experience levels. The routes in Meis are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 600 reviews. More than 5,800 hikers have used komoot to explore Meis's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Meis?
A: There are over 220 hiking routes around Meis, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. The komoot community has explored more than 5,800 of these routes.

Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Meis?
A: Hiking around Meis features diverse landscapes, including river valleys, forested areas, and moderate elevations. Trails often follow waterways, leading through woodlands and past historical sites like monasteries and traditional mills, showcasing the natural and cultural heritage of Galicia.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Meis?
A: Yes, Meis offers over 100 easy hiking trails. These routes are generally shorter and have less elevation gain, making them suitable for beginners or families looking for a relaxed outdoor experience. Many trails follow river paths, providing gentle gradients.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Meis?
A: Several circular routes are available in Meis. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375590788/el-bosque-de-colon-to-alto-de-castrove-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Bosque de Colón to Alto de Castrove — circular&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 6.4-mile (10.3 km) option, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375609046/armenteira-to-meano-circular-by-the-molinos-rio-de-chanca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Armenteira to Meaño — circular by the Molinos río de Chanca&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate 9.6-mile (15.5 km) loop.

Q: What are some interesting natural attractions or landmarks to see near the hiking trails?
A: Hikers can discover several interesting natural attractions and landmarks. Notable spots include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2030121" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barosa Waterfall and Mills&lt;/a&gt;, the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4725398" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casto San Pedro Gardens&lt;/a&gt;, and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6701417" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Stone and Water Route&lt;/a&gt;, which features traditional mills along the river.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Meis?
A: Many trails in Meis are dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy the natural surroundings with your pet. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash and follow local regulations regarding pet access in natural areas.

Q: What is the average duration of hikes in Meis?
A: Hike durations in Meis vary widely depending on the route's length and difficulty. For instance, a moderate trail like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375590788/el-bosque-de-colon-to-alto-de-castrove-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Bosque de Colón to Alto de Castrove — circular&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 3 hours, while longer routes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375609046/armenteira-to-meano-circular-by-the-molinos-rio-de-chanca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Armenteira to Meaño — circular by the Molinos río de Chanca&lt;/a&gt; can take over 4.5 hours.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay near the hiking areas, especially around Armenteira Monastery?
A: The area around Meis, including the vicinity of the Armenteira Monastery, offers various local establishments. While specific cafes, pubs, or accommodations are not detailed in the guide, being in Galicia, Spain, you can expect to find local eateries and guesthouses in nearby villages to cater to hikers.

Q: Is public transport available to access hiking trails in Meis?
A: Information on specific public transport routes directly to trailheads in Meis is not readily available. It is generally advisable to check local bus schedules or consider driving to access the more remote starting points of the hiking trails in this region of Galicia.

Q: Where can I park my car when hiking in Meis?
A: While specific parking locations are not detailed for every trailhead, most popular hiking areas and trailheads in regions like Meis typically have designated parking spots or roadside parking available. It is recommended to check the starting point of your chosen route on komoot for any specific parking instructions or nearby facilities.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Meis?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 600 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, the peaceful river valleys, and the cultural landmarks like traditional mills and monasteries that are integrated into the hiking experience.
tips: Of Renaissance and Baroque style, from the 16th and 17th centuries, the magnificent Romanesque church built by its first Cistercian abbot in 1167 is also preserved. It has a Latin cross plan with three naves and three apses. The cover is perhaps the most remarkable of the temple, with its six archivolts supported on as many pairs of columns and above, a beautiful openwork rose window.

A magical and easy hiking route through the waters of the Chanca River. The PR-G 172 route of the Chanca River runs through the municipalities of Meaño and Meis. Explore the river landscape surrounded by native trees.
